query: Is there a required amount of ECTS I have to reach or I lose my place?

queries: Is there a required amount of ECTS I have to reach or I lose my place?

Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits? If you were enrolled for your exchange program in the winter semester of 2022/23 or later and now apply for admission to the the same bachelor's or diploma programme, you will need to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters (including the semester(s) of your exchange). - Do I also have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes? retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: For your information
retrieved.text:
If you study full-time, you are expected to obtain 120 ECTS credits within four semesters according to the curriculum. The majority of students achieves 16 ECTS credits within one semester, for example by completing the introductory and orientation period (STEOP).

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

- students with proven physical/mental impairments
- bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you have been admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23
- master’s and doctoral programmes

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

retrieved.title: Extension curricula and alternative extensions
retrieved.text:
Almost all bachelor’s programmes require students to complete extension curricula (EC) in addition to the regular compulsory and elective subjects. An EC is a predifined module group of up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits is defined in the curriculum of your degree programme.

In order to complete an EC, you must first register for the EC in [u:space](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/#c216328). You will be able to register for individual courses of the EC after having passed the STEOP.

Nearly all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless otherwise specified, you may freely choose from the range of EC offered.

You can also complete [alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c273245) of a number of at least 15 ECTS instead of an EC in most bachelor's degree programmes..

- If you have questions about the content of an EC and the recognition of examination results from an EC, please get in touch with the [studies service unit (SSS)/studies service center (SSC)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for the EC.
- If you have questions about the recognition of an EC for your degree programme, please contact the [SSS/SSC](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme.

- [Alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228260)
- [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c216354)
- [Extension curricula (EC)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228433)

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters (including the semester(s) of your exchange). - Do I also have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes? No, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes. Achievements completed in complementary degree programmes are not recognised for the minimum number of credits. - I am enrolled in the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits for each teaching subject separately? What happens if I change my teaching subject? In the bachelor’s programme in Teacher Education, you have to obtain the minimum number of credits for both teaching subjects and the pedagogical part in total. You do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits for each teaching subject separately. If one teaching subject in your combination changes, as long as one of the two teaching subjects remains the same, the semesters continue to be counted for the new combination. Both newly obtained ECTS credits and ECTS credits recognised in due time are counting towards the ECTS credits for the new combination. If you already obtained the minimum number of credits for the old combination, you do not have to obtain them for the new one again. If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits in due time, you

retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
retrieved.text:
You are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law if you

- were admitted to a jurisprudential degree programme at a recognised educational institution and
- if you can prove having achieved at least 60 ECTS.

The 60 ECTS have to be recognisable for compulsory or elective subjects or compulsory modules of the diploma programme in Law at the University of Vienna. The StudiesServiceCenter Law has created an [overview about recognitions](https://ssc-rechtswissenschaften.univie.ac.at/diplomstudium-neu-2025/anerkennung/) (only available in German). Please check beforehand if your achievements can be recognised.

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. For studies that you started before the winter semester of 2022/23 and have not yet interrupted, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits. Update from 29 September 2022 - How are ECTS credits counted when I resume my studies? If you resume your studies within the same curriculum, the counting of the ECTS credits continues after resumption. If you interrupt your studies and later resume your studies in a succeeding curriculum, or if you change a teaching subject in your combination, your previously obtained ECTS credits must be recognised to ensure that they count towards the minimum number of credits. If you still need ECTS credits for the minimum number of credits, you have to obtain them within the remaining time. - What happens if I do not obtain the minimum number of credits? If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits (16 ECTS credits) for a degree programme within the first four semesters, your admission to the degree programme expires on 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all bachelor's and diploma programmes who begin or [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) their studies in the winter semester of 2022/2023 or later must obtain the minimum number of credits. The minimum number of credits must be obtained once within four semesters. If you interrupt your studies (again) during these four semesters, the ECTS credits and semesters are counted after you resume your studies. However, once the minimum number of credits has been obtained, it does not have to be obtained again for this degree programme. For example: You started your studies in the winter semester of 2022/23, studied for 2 semesters and obtained 12 ECTS credits. You interrupt your studies in the following semester. You resume your studies in the summer semester of 2024. You still have 2 semesters to obtain the missing 4 ECTS credits - i.e. until one month after the end of the fourth semester at the latest. If you interrupt your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. For studies that you started before the winter semester of 2022/23 and have not

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: E
retrieved.text: / - ECTS credits The "European Credit Transfer System (ECTS)" contributes to the mobility between degree programmes, universities and countries, as ECTS credits facilitate a comparison of academic achievements. ECTS credits describe the average workload required to achieve the objectives of a course, module or degree programme. One ECTS credit corresponds to an average workload of 25 full hours. Course participation as well as the preparation and follow-up of a course, the duration of the exam and studying time are considered as part of the workload. In u:find you can see how many ECTS credits you have to obtain to successfully complete a course. Example: Max attends a seminar comprising 4 ECTS credits (= 100 hours). The seminar takes place ten times and takes 1.5 hours each time (= 15 hours). He has to prepare a presentation and a summary (which takes him 30 hours), a seminar paper (30 hours) and he has to read texts for every second session (5 hours each). Over the course of the semester, he will be working 100 hours for the seminar. Related Links: - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Bachelor programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30829&cHash=c027deec5ec3cafecd595fd8e14cc288) - [Prescribed duration of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=42309&cHash=54e30ba32dac123141345da256f85101) - Educational institution ID The educational institution ID is a unique label of a higher education institution and precedes the degree programme code. Both can be found in the Study overview on u:space and on the

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. What are the requirements for my studies? If at least one subject in a teacher education combination is completed at a university of art in Vienna, the respective university of art is responsible for calculating the ECTS credits. The ECTS credits are calculated over the entire duration of the degree programme. Information about exams at the University of Vienna will be obtained if necessary. If students have completed less than 16 ECTS credits by the end of the fourth semester (deadline 31 October for the summer semester/31 March for the winter semester), it is up to the respective university of art to exclude the student from the degree programme after reviewing all information. ECTS credits obtained at the University of Vienna are displayed in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). - Are there any programs that support me in my studies and in achieving the minimum number of credits? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: combination. If you already obtained the minimum number of credits for the old combination, you do not have to obtain them for the new one again. If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits in due time, you will be excluded from this programme for 4 semesters. You are excluded from those teaching subjects for which you have already been admitted for 4 semesters. The combination with a teaching subject for which you have been admitted for less than 4 semesters is possible. In this case the semesters that you have already completed and the ECTS credits from the previous subject combination that can be used for the new subject combination are counted. Example 1: You interrupt the teacher education programme after 3 semesters and resume your studies sometime later (with at least one teaching subject remaining the same): You have 1 semester left to obtain the required 16 ECTS credits. Example 2: You changed one teaching subject in the 3rd semester. You were not able to obtain the minimum number of credits within 4 semesters and are excluded from the teacher education programme. You can study the teaching subject that you only studied for one semester for another 3 semesters in combination with another teaching subject to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits. ECTS credits obtained in the first semester that can also be used

retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
Follow these steps:
retrieved.text:
- [#### Step 1: Send an e-mail to the SSC Law

  Send your proof of the 60 ECTS and a scan of your personal ID via e-mail to the StudiesServiceCenter Law no later than 5 February 2026. By this time you must have already completed the 60 ECTS. The StudiesServiceCenter will evaluate your documents. You will receive a confirmation via e-mail.

  If less than 60 ECTS credits can be recognised, no exemption can be made. You will receive the relevant information via e-mail. In this case, you must successfully complete the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law.

  Message the SSC](#)
- [#### Step 2: create a u:account

  Create your u:account in u:space no later than the deadline. Make sure you remember the login data, because you'll also need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space and complete the registration.

  Go to u:space

  ©
  Ian Ehm](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en)
- [#### Step 3: Submit the application for admission

  Send the following documents via the Servicedesk no later than th deadline:

  - the confirmation of the StudiesServiceCenter Law
  - your secondary-school leaving certificate (all pages!)
    - including a translation (for certificates that were not issued in German or English)
    - including a legalisation (if required for your country)
  - proof of German language proficiency at level C1

  Afterwards you will receive further information via e-mail.

  Message us via the Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
retrieved.text:
You have to obtain at least 16 ECTS credits in every bachelor’s and diploma programme within four semesters.

This applies

- to all programmes to which you are admitted since the winter semester of 2022/2023
- and to all programmes that you [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) from this time.

Otherwise, the admission to the degree programme expires. If you would like to apply for re-admission to the same degree programme (= resumption of studies) at the University of Vienna, you can only do so after two [academic years](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/) (= four semesters).

heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: teaching subject that you only studied for one semester for another 3 semesters in combination with another teaching subject to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits. ECTS credits obtained in the first semester that can also be used for the new subject combination are counted. Example 3: You were not able to obtain the minimum number of credits within 4 semesters and are excluded from the teacher education programme. You begin a teacher education programme with a completely different combination of teaching subjects. The counting of ECTS credits and semesters for the minimum number of credits starts at 0 again. - I am studying a complementary teacher education programme. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits? No, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes. If you include a teaching subject from a complementary teacher education programme in your subject combination, only the semesters from previous subject combinations of the newly combined subjects are counted. Example: You are studying a subject combination and a complementary degree programme at the same time. After 3 semesters, you exchange one of your teaching subjects with the subject of your complementary degree programme. You have to obtain the minimum number of credits within the remaining fourth semester of the combination programme. Exams completed in the complementary degree programme count, if you completed

heading_path: P
retrieved.text: you did not allocate are lost. After the end of the registration period and the subsequent allocation of places, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail-address informing you about the status of your registration. For further information on the registration procedure, please contact the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) or StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme. Example: Max would like to attend a course with 25 available places. A total number of 30 students register for this course. Max allocates 200 points to this course. As 27 other students allocated between 210 and 500 points, he does not get a place. 70 students register for another course with 50 available places. Max allocates 100 points and gets a place, because several students allocated fewer points than he did. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) Related entries: - [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31232&cHash=71ef139afe33c7e9cb44ca0bdf597ad4) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31341&cHash=5d15ccad6a59f9bd9dad3b073a06673e) - [Allocation of available places](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31956&cHash=cd316add293db7beb7397f54a0ba5b64) - [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32965&cHash=f6d48ac305556ae51a0a3869bf9a2e10) - Positive assessment Your grade will be entered within four weeks after the exam/the completion of a course or final partial achievement. You can check your grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module).

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: E
retrieved.text: your own degree programme, but also extension curricula (EC). An extension curriculum is a predefined module group comprising up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits for extension curricula is defined in your degree programme’s curriculum. Almost all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless specified otherwise in your degree programme’s curriculum, you can, to a great extent, freely choose from the entire range of extension curricula. Example: Max is studying on the bachelor’s programme in Sociology, for which he has to complete extension curricula comprising 30 ECTS credits. The curriculum does not specify which extension curricula he has to attend, but he can not choose EC from his degree programme. Related Links: - [List of extension curricula (EC)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/)

Please note the application deadlines, as well as the consequences of taking a leave of absence. [All information about taking a leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) Option 3: You do not want to continue your studies? Close your degree programme. Before closing, inform yourself about the possible implications for re-enrolment. You will get detailed information about the course type, number of ECTS credits, registration and de-registration periods, course dates, contents, Moodle, lecturers, etc. Please note that you can only register for a course or exam within the registration period. retrieved.title: Planning your semester
heading_path: This is how it works:
retrieved.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: FAQ
retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

retrieved.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
retrieved.text:
The admission to master programmes is possible with supplementary examinations to an extent of up to 30 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. If this extent is exceeded, your application for admission will be rejected.

Your application for admission was rejected for this reason? You can make up for lacking supplementary examinations in a non-degree programme, until 30 ECTS or less remain. For this purpose, you will be admitted to "attending individual courses", not to a specific degree programme.

For example: You lack 39 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. Therefore, your application is rejected. Subsequently, you complete supplementary examinations to an extent of 9 ECTS or more as a non-degree student.  Afterwards, you can apply for admission to the degree programme again within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

If the supplementary examination in German was prescribed in your admission letter, please read the information regarding the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) carefully.

- valid passport or personal ID card in original form

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

You can also complete [alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c273245) of a number of at least 15 ECTS instead of an EC in most bachelor's degree programmes..

- If you have questions about the content of an EC and the recognition of examination results from an EC, please get in touch with the [studies service unit (SSS)/studies service center (SSC)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for the EC.
- If you have questions about the recognition of an EC for your degree programme, please contact the [SSS/SSC](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme.

- [Alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228260)
- [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c216354)
- [Extension curricula (EC)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228433)

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters (including the semester(s) of your exchange). - Do I also have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes? No, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes. Achievements completed in complementary degree programmes are not recognised for the minimum number of credits. - I am enrolled in the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits for each teaching subject separately? What happens if I change my teaching subject? In the bachelor’s programme in Teacher Education, you have to obtain the minimum number of credits for both teaching subjects and the pedagogical part in total. You do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits for each teaching subject separately. If one teaching subject in your combination changes, as long as one of the two teaching subjects remains the same, the semesters continue to be counted for the new combination. Both newly obtained ECTS credits and ECTS credits recognised in due time are counting towards the ECTS credits for the new combination. If you already obtained the minimum number of credits for the old combination, you do not have to obtain them for the new one again. If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits in due time, you

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
reranked.text:
You are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law if you

- were admitted to a jurisprudential degree programme at a recognised educational institution and
- if you can prove having achieved at least 60 ECTS.

The 60 ECTS have to be recognisable for compulsory or elective subjects or compulsory modules of the diploma programme in Law at the University of Vienna. The StudiesServiceCenter Law has created an [overview about recognitions](https://ssc-rechtswissenschaften.univie.ac.at/diplomstudium-neu-2025/anerkennung/) (only available in German). Please check beforehand if your achievements can be recognised.

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. For studies that you started before the winter semester of 2022/23 and have not yet interrupted, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits. Update from 29 September 2022 - How are ECTS credits counted when I resume my studies? If you resume your studies within the same curriculum, the counting of the ECTS credits continues after resumption. If you interrupt your studies and later resume your studies in a succeeding curriculum, or if you change a teaching subject in your combination, your previously obtained ECTS credits must be recognised to ensure that they count towards the minimum number of credits. If you still need ECTS credits for the minimum number of credits, you have to obtain them within the remaining time. - What happens if I do not obtain the minimum number of credits? If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits (16 ECTS credits) for a degree programme within the first four semesters, your admission to the degree programme expires on 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all bachelor's and diploma programmes who begin or [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) their studies in the winter semester of 2022/2023 or later must obtain the minimum number of credits. The minimum number of credits must be obtained once within four semesters. If you interrupt your studies (again) during these four semesters, the ECTS credits and semesters are counted after you resume your studies. However, once the minimum number of credits has been obtained, it does not have to be obtained again for this degree programme. For example: You started your studies in the winter semester of 2022/23, studied for 2 semesters and obtained 12 ECTS credits. You interrupt your studies in the following semester. You resume your studies in the summer semester of 2024. You still have 2 semesters to obtain the missing 4 ECTS credits - i.e. until one month after the end of the fourth semester at the latest. If you interrupt your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. For studies that you started before the winter semester of 2022/23 and have not

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: E
reranked.text: / - ECTS credits The "European Credit Transfer System (ECTS)" contributes to the mobility between degree programmes, universities and countries, as ECTS credits facilitate a comparison of academic achievements. ECTS credits describe the average workload required to achieve the objectives of a course, module or degree programme. One ECTS credit corresponds to an average workload of 25 full hours. Course participation as well as the preparation and follow-up of a course, the duration of the exam and studying time are considered as part of the workload. In u:find you can see how many ECTS credits you have to obtain to successfully complete a course. Example: Max attends a seminar comprising 4 ECTS credits (= 100 hours). The seminar takes place ten times and takes 1.5 hours each time (= 15 hours). He has to prepare a presentation and a summary (which takes him 30 hours), a seminar paper (30 hours) and he has to read texts for every second session (5 hours each). Over the course of the semester, he will be working 100 hours for the seminar. Related Links: - [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html) Related entries: - [Bachelor programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30829&cHash=c027deec5ec3cafecd595fd8e14cc288) - [Prescribed duration of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=42309&cHash=54e30ba32dac123141345da256f85101) - Educational institution ID The educational institution ID is a unique label of a higher education institution and precedes the degree programme code. Both can be found in the Study overview on u:space and on the

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: time. If you have completed a course/exam multiple times, only the date of the last examination result counts. - I study at least one of my two teaching subjects at a university of art in Vienna. What are the requirements for my studies? If at least one subject in a teacher education combination is completed at a university of art in Vienna, the respective university of art is responsible for calculating the ECTS credits. The ECTS credits are calculated over the entire duration of the degree programme. Information about exams at the University of Vienna will be obtained if necessary. If students have completed less than 16 ECTS credits by the end of the fourth semester (deadline 31 October for the summer semester/31 March for the winter semester), it is up to the respective university of art to exclude the student from the degree programme after reviewing all information. ECTS credits obtained at the University of Vienna are displayed in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). - Are there any programs that support me in my studies and in achieving the minimum number of credits? This is how you can progress in your studies: - Plan your semester. - Study continuously. - Use the study materials on Moodle. - Form study groups. - Make us of tutorials. - Take exams. [Information about Semesterplanning](/en/semester-planning/) Make use of the [services for students](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) to achieve your goal. The

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: combination. If you already obtained the minimum number of credits for the old combination, you do not have to obtain them for the new one again. If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits in due time, you will be excluded from this programme for 4 semesters. You are excluded from those teaching subjects for which you have already been admitted for 4 semesters. The combination with a teaching subject for which you have been admitted for less than 4 semesters is possible. In this case the semesters that you have already completed and the ECTS credits from the previous subject combination that can be used for the new subject combination are counted. Example 1: You interrupt the teacher education programme after 3 semesters and resume your studies sometime later (with at least one teaching subject remaining the same): You have 1 semester left to obtain the required 16 ECTS credits. Example 2: You changed one teaching subject in the 3rd semester. You were not able to obtain the minimum number of credits within 4 semesters and are excluded from the teacher education programme. You can study the teaching subject that you only studied for one semester for another 3 semesters in combination with another teaching subject to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits. ECTS credits obtained in the first semester that can also be used

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
heading_path: 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
Follow these steps:
reranked.text:
- [#### Step 1: Send an e-mail to the SSC Law

  Send your proof of the 60 ECTS and a scan of your personal ID via e-mail to the StudiesServiceCenter Law no later than 5 February 2026. By this time you must have already completed the 60 ECTS. The StudiesServiceCenter will evaluate your documents. You will receive a confirmation via e-mail.

  If less than 60 ECTS credits can be recognised, no exemption can be made. You will receive the relevant information via e-mail. In this case, you must successfully complete the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law.

  Message the SSC](#)
- [#### Step 2: create a u:account

  Create your u:account in u:space no later than the deadline. Make sure you remember the login data, because you'll also need the u:account during your studies. Log in to u:space and complete the registration.

  Go to u:space

  ©
  Ian Ehm](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en)
- [#### Step 3: Submit the application for admission

  Send the following documents via the Servicedesk no later than th deadline:

  - the confirmation of the StudiesServiceCenter Law
  - your secondary-school leaving certificate (all pages!)
    - including a translation (for certificates that were not issued in German or English)
    - including a legalisation (if required for your country)
  - proof of German language proficiency at level C1

  Afterwards you will receive further information via e-mail.

  Message us via the Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
reranked.text:
You have to obtain at least 16 ECTS credits in every bachelor’s and diploma programme within four semesters.

This applies

- to all programmes to which you are admitted since the winter semester of 2022/2023
- and to all programmes that you [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) from this time.

Otherwise, the admission to the degree programme expires. If you would like to apply for re-admission to the same degree programme (= resumption of studies) at the University of Vienna, you can only do so after two [academic years](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/) (= four semesters).

reranked.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
reranked.text: teaching subject that you only studied for one semester for another 3 semesters in combination with another teaching subject to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits. ECTS credits obtained in the first semester that can also be used for the new subject combination are counted. Example 3: You were not able to obtain the minimum number of credits within 4 semesters and are excluded from the teacher education programme. You begin a teacher education programme with a completely different combination of teaching subjects. The counting of ECTS credits and semesters for the minimum number of credits starts at 0 again. - I am studying a complementary teacher education programme. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits? No, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes. If you include a teaching subject from a complementary teacher education programme in your subject combination, only the semesters from previous subject combinations of the newly combined subjects are counted. Example: You are studying a subject combination and a complementary degree programme at the same time. After 3 semesters, you exchange one of your teaching subjects with the subject of your complementary degree programme. You have to obtain the minimum number of credits within the remaining fourth semester of the combination programme. Exams completed in the complementary degree programme count, if you completed

heading_path: P
reranked.text: you did not allocate are lost. After the end of the registration period and the subsequent allocation of places, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail-address informing you about the status of your registration. For further information on the registration procedure, please contact the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) or StudiesServiceCenter of your degree programme. Example: Max would like to attend a course with 25 available places. A total number of 30 students register for this course. Max allocates 200 points to this course. As 27 other students allocated between 210 and 500 points, he does not get a place. 70 students register for another course with 50 available places. Max allocates 100 points and gets a place, because several students allocated fewer points than he did. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) Related entries: - [Preference system](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31232&cHash=71ef139afe33c7e9cb44ca0bdf597ad4) - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - [Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31341&cHash=5d15ccad6a59f9bd9dad3b073a06673e) - [Allocation of available places](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31956&cHash=cd316add293db7beb7397f54a0ba5b64) - [Recommended path of study](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32965&cHash=f6d48ac305556ae51a0a3869bf9a2e10) - Positive assessment Your grade will be entered within four weeks after the exam/the completion of a course or final partial achievement. You can check your grade in the record of examinations in u:space as well as in your transcript of records. You can re-sit a passed exam once within twelve months (provided that you have not yet completed your degree programme/stage of the degree programme/module).

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: E
reranked.text: your own degree programme, but also extension curricula (EC). An extension curriculum is a predefined module group comprising up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits for extension curricula is defined in your degree programme’s curriculum. Almost all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless specified otherwise in your degree programme’s curriculum, you can, to a great extent, freely choose from the entire range of extension curricula. Example: Max is studying on the bachelor’s programme in Sociology, for which he has to complete extension curricula comprising 30 ECTS credits. The curriculum does not specify which extension curricula he has to attend, but he can not choose EC from his degree programme. Related Links: - [List of extension curricula (EC)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/)

You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply for re-admission to the same degree programme at the University of Vienna. - What options do I have if it becomes apparent that I will not be able to achieve the minimum number of credits? Option 1: Try everything you can and make use of the following offers and information: - [Registration for courses and exams](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/semesterplanung/anmeldung-zu-lehrveranstaltungenpruefungen/) - [Support services provided by the University of Vienna for successful studying](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/) - [Psychological Counselling Service for students: counselling for study-related and personal issues](https://www.studentenberatung.at/) - [Information for students with physical/mental impairments](/en/accessible-studies/) - [Student life - Who can help me with...?](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/) Option 2: If you can claim one of the precisely defined reasons, apply for a leave of absence. Please note the application deadlines, as well as the consequences of taking a leave of absence. [All information about taking a leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) Option 3: You do not want to continue your studies? Close your degree programme. Before closing, inform yourself about the possible implications for re-enrolment. [All information about closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - Where can I check the ECTS credits obtained for my studies? For each ongoing degree

To find out which courses you can attend for the curriculum point you have not yet completed, either click on "Link to u:find" at the book icon or search directly in the Course Directory u:find for courses suitable for the curriculum point. Alternatively, you can search for modules that you have not yet completed in the semester planner in u:space (completed = green tick). There you will find all the courses currently offered for the module. Use your [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula) to check for which curriculum point the course can be recognised. - Step 3: Select course in u:find Click on the courses available for completing the selected achievement in the Course Directory [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html). You will get detailed information about the course type, number of ECTS credits, registration and de-registration periods, course dates, contents, Moodle, lecturers, etc. Please note that you can only register for a course or exam within the registration period. If you click on “Register/Deregister for this course” you will be automatically forwarded to u:space where you can complete your registration. - Step 4: Register for a pi LV After Step 3: “Select course in u:find” you will be forwarded to the registration system in u:space. Please select your degree programme and the respective curriculum point (module). (If there are several curriculum points available, select the

reranked.title: Planning your semester
heading_path: This is how it works:
reranked.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: FAQ
reranked.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

reranked.title: Making up for supplementary examinations from the bachelor programme for admission to a master programme
reranked.text:
The admission to master programmes is possible with supplementary examinations to an extent of up to 30 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. If this extent is exceeded, your application for admission will be rejected.

Your application for admission was rejected for this reason? You can make up for lacking supplementary examinations in a non-degree programme, until 30 ECTS or less remain. For this purpose, you will be admitted to "attending individual courses", not to a specific degree programme.

For example: You lack 39 ECTS to compensate for fundamental subject-specific differences. Therefore, your application is rejected. Subsequently, you complete supplementary examinations to an extent of 9 ECTS or more as a non-degree student.  Afterwards, you can apply for admission to the degree programme again within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/).

If the supplementary examination in German was prescribed in your admission letter, please read the information regarding the [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) carefully.

- valid passport or personal ID card in original form

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application and admission period: from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application and admission period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

For each ongoing degree programme affected, you can see the number of ECTS credits already obtained for the minimum number of credits in your [u:space dashboard](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). The data are updated automatically 4 times a day. - I am continuing my studies (without interruption) that have been changed to a new curriculum. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits again? No. A conversion to the successor curriculum is considered a continuation of the previous degree programme. - I have already studied at the University of Vienna through an exchange programme (e.g. Erasmus+, Erasmus+ Blended Intensive Programme, Non-EU Exchange) and would now like to enrol as a regular student. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits? If you were enrolled for your exchange program in the winter semester of 2022/23 or later and now apply for admission to the the same bachelor's or diploma programme, you will need to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters (including the semester(s) of your exchange). - Do I also have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes? No, you do not have to obtain the

selected.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: For your information
selected.text:
If you study full-time, you are expected to obtain 120 ECTS credits within four semesters according to the curriculum. The majority of students achieves 16 ECTS credits within one semester, for example by completing the introductory and orientation period (STEOP).

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

- students with proven physical/mental impairments
- bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you have been admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23
- master’s and doctoral programmes

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

selected.title: Extension curricula and alternative extensions
selected.text:
Almost all bachelor’s programmes require students to complete extension curricula (EC) in addition to the regular compulsory and elective subjects. An EC is a predifined module group of up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits is defined in the curriculum of your degree programme.

In order to complete an EC, you must first register for the EC in [u:space](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/#c216328). You will be able to register for individual courses of the EC after having passed the STEOP.

Nearly all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless otherwise specified, you may freely choose from the range of EC offered.

You can also complete [alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c273245) of a number of at least 15 ECTS instead of an EC in most bachelor's degree programmes..

- If you have questions about the content of an EC and the recognition of examination results from an EC, please get in touch with the [studies service unit (SSS)/studies service center (SSC)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for the EC.
- If you have questions about the recognition of an EC for your degree programme, please contact the [SSS/SSC](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme.

- [Alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228260)
- [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c216354)
- [Extension curricula (EC)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228433)

If you resume your studies within the same curriculum, the counting of the ECTS credits continues after resumption. If you interrupt your studies and later resume your studies in a succeeding curriculum, or if you change a teaching subject in your combination, your previously obtained ECTS credits must be recognised to ensure that they count towards the minimum number of credits. If you still need ECTS credits for the minimum number of credits, you have to obtain them within the remaining time. - What happens if I do not obtain the minimum number of credits? If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits (16 ECTS credits) for a degree programme within the first four semesters, your admission to the degree programme expires on 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply

assemble.context:
[1] Minimum number of credits — FAQs about the minimum number of credits
degree programme. Before closing, inform yourself about the possible implications for re-enrolment. [All information about closing a degree programme/withdrawing from the University of Vienna](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) - Where can I check the ECTS credits obtained for my studies? For each ongoing degree programme affected, you can see the number of ECTS credits already obtained for the minimum number of credits in your [u:space dashboard](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium). The data are updated automatically 4 times a day. - I am continuing my studies (without interruption) that have been changed to a new curriculum. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits again? No. A conversion to the successor curriculum is considered a continuation of the previous degree programme. - I have already studied at the University of Vienna through an exchange programme (e.g. Erasmus+, Erasmus+ Blended Intensive Programme, Non-EU Exchange) and would now like to enrol as a regular student. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits? If you were enrolled for your exchange program in the winter semester of 2022/23 or later and now apply for admission to the the same bachelor's or diploma programme, you will need to obtain the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters (including the semester(s) of your exchange). - Do I also have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes? No, you do not have to obtain the

[2] Minimum number of credits — For your information
If you study full-time, you are expected to obtain 120 ECTS credits within four semesters according to the curriculum. The majority of students achieves 16 ECTS credits within one semester, for example by completing the introductory and orientation period (STEOP).

There is no mandatory study pace apart from the minimum number of credits. You decide yourself how fast you study and how many ECTS credits you complete per semester. However, please note that financial aids and scholarships may be subject to stricter rules.

The minimum number of credits does not apply to

- students with proven physical/mental impairments
- bachelor’s or diploma programmes to which you have been admitted before the winter semester of 2022/23
- master’s and doctoral programmes

Information about the [recognition of the additional exam Latin](https://klassischephilologie.univie.ac.at/studium/ergaenzungspruefungen/latein-ergaenzungspruefung/) for the minimum number of credits can be found (in German only) on the website of the Institute for Classical Philology, Medieval and Modern Latin.

[3] Extension curricula and alternative extensions
Almost all bachelor’s programmes require students to complete extension curricula (EC) in addition to the regular compulsory and elective subjects. An EC is a predifined module group of up to 30 ECTS credits. The required number of ECTS credits is defined in the curriculum of your degree programme.

In order to complete an EC, you must first register for the EC in [u:space](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/#c216328). You will be able to register for individual courses of the EC after having passed the STEOP.

Nearly all degree programmes offer extension curricula. Unless otherwise specified, you may freely choose from the range of EC offered.

You can also complete [alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c273245) of a number of at least 15 ECTS instead of an EC in most bachelor's degree programmes..

- If you have questions about the content of an EC and the recognition of examination results from an EC, please get in touch with the [studies service unit (SSS)/studies service center (SSC)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for the EC.
- If you have questions about the recognition of an EC for your degree programme, please contact the [SSS/SSC](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme.

- [Alternative extensions (AE)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228260)
- [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c216354)
- [Extension curricula (EC)](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c228433)

[4] Minimum number of credits — FAQs about the minimum number of credits
the minimum number of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters (including the semester(s) of your exchange). - Do I also have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes? No, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits in complementary degree programmes. Achievements completed in complementary degree programmes are not recognised for the minimum number of credits. - I am enrolled in the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education. Do I have to obtain the minimum number of credits for each teaching subject separately? What happens if I change my teaching subject? In the bachelor’s programme in Teacher Education, you have to obtain the minimum number of credits for both teaching subjects and the pedagogical part in total. You do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits for each teaching subject separately. If one teaching subject in your combination changes, as long as one of the two teaching subjects remains the same, the semesters continue to be counted for the new combination. Both newly obtained ECTS credits and ECTS credits recognised in due time are counting towards the ECTS credits for the new combination. If you already obtained the minimum number of credits for the old combination, you do not have to obtain them for the new one again. If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits in due time, you

[5] Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure — 60 ECTS exemption for the diploma programme in Law
You are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the diploma programme in Law if you

- were admitted to a jurisprudential degree programme at a recognised educational institution and
- if you can prove having achieved at least 60 ECTS.

The 60 ECTS have to be recognisable for compulsory or elective subjects or compulsory modules of the diploma programme in Law at the University of Vienna. The StudiesServiceCenter Law has created an [overview about recognitions](https://ssc-rechtswissenschaften.univie.ac.at/diplomstudium-neu-2025/anerkennung/) (only available in German). Please check beforehand if your achievements can be recognised.

[6] Minimum number of credits — FAQs about the minimum number of credits
your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. For studies that you started before the winter semester of 2022/23 and have not yet interrupted, you do not have to obtain the minimum number of credits. Update from 29 September 2022 - How are ECTS credits counted when I resume my studies? If you resume your studies within the same curriculum, the counting of the ECTS credits continues after resumption. If you interrupt your studies and later resume your studies in a succeeding curriculum, or if you change a teaching subject in your combination, your previously obtained ECTS credits must be recognised to ensure that they count towards the minimum number of credits. If you still need ECTS credits for the minimum number of credits, you have to obtain them within the remaining time. - What happens if I do not obtain the minimum number of credits? If you do not obtain the minimum number of credits (16 ECTS credits) for a degree programme within the first four semesters, your admission to the degree programme expires on 31 October or 31 March, depending on whether the fourth semester is the summer or the winter semester. You will be excluded from this programme for four semesters. Once four semesters have passed, you can apply

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Question: Is there a required amount of ECTS I have to reach or I lose my place?

generate.raw_response: Yes, you must obtain a minimum of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters [1][2][4][6]. If you do not, your admission to the degree programme expires and you will be excluded from the programme for four semesters [6].

answer: Yes, you must obtain a minimum of 16 ECTS credits within four semesters [1][2][4][6]. If you do not, your admission to the degree programme expires and you will be excluded from the programme for four semesters [6].
